content:
The derivative emerged from **Newton** and **Leibniz** in the 17th century to solve problems of motion and tangents—unifying instantaneous velocity with slope of a curve.

**Euler** and later analysts formalized limits and made differentiation rigorous, replacing informal infinitesimals.

In the 20th century, derivatives became central to optimization theory (**Lagrange**, **KKT** conditions) and differential equations.

Modern machine learning treats derivatives as operational quantities computed billions of times per second via automatic differentiation during training of neural networks and generative models.

timeline.label: 17th century
timeline.description: Newton and Leibniz develop calculus and rules for derivatives.

timeline.label: 19th century rigor
timeline.description: Limit definitions formalize what derivatives mean analytically.

timeline.label: Optimization theory
timeline.description: Derivatives characterize optima and underpin gradient methods.

timeline.label: Automatic differentiation
timeline.description: Frameworks compute exact derivatives of program expressions for ML training.
